summary: Fire‑charged second set with soaring Watchtower and tight jams
blurb: The show delivers a standout second set highlighted by an out‑of‑this‑world drum/space sequence and a soaring All Along The Watchtower. Jerry’s guitar shines, Brent’s keys sparkle, and the band rides high on High Time and Ship Of Fools. The encore closes with a powerful Black Muddy River vocal.
review: Red Rocks on August 11, 1987 offered a quintessential late‑'80s Dead experience: a solid first set that built momentum into a second set that truly ignited the canyon walls. After a bright opener of Cold Rain And Snow and Little Red Rooster, the band launched into High Time and When I Paint My Masterpiece before pushing the energy higher with Push Comes To Shove and Let It Grow. The real fireworks began after Crazy Fingers when Samson & Delilah segued into Ship Of Fools and then exploded into a blistering Playing In The Band → China Doll transition that fed directly into an unforgettable drum/space climax, culminating in a soaring All Along The Watchtower and a raucous Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad. Jerry’s guitar tone cut through the Red Rocks acoustics with clarity—sometimes thin in the mix but always full of bite—and his vocal sign‑off on Black Muddy River gave the encore an emotional high point. Brent’s keyboards were pouncy, errie and perfectly balanced, providing highlights on High Time, Masterpiece and Tons Of Steel, while Mickey and Bill delivered thunderous, heavy‑hitting drumming that drove the show forward.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 out of 6) are marked positive, repeatedly praising standout songs (Watchtower, Ship Of Fools, High Time, etc.), strong band interplay and energetic drum/space sections. Only one recording is mixed with no consistent negative commentary, so the overall sentiment is strongly positive.

best_recording.reason: Matrix mix with sweet balance and crowd ambience; captures the standout Watchtower/Ship Of Fools sequence clearly while preserving the venue’s energy.

key_highlights: Soaring All Along The Watchtower performance in the second set
Thunderous drum/space segment that drives the show forward
Brent's pouncy, errie piano solos—especially on High Time and Masterpiece
Tight transitions from Playing In The Band into China Doll
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Bright, clear guitar tone that occasionally sounds thin in the mix but remains powerful; delivers a strong vocal sign‑off on Black Muddy River and adds extra energy early with "boppin' around".
band_performance.Phil: Clean, prominent bass work with an especially impressive bomb at the end jam of the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar throughout; shines on Masterpiece and provides memorable vocals on Little Red Rooster, with audience chanting "Playin Bob!" during the show.
band_performance.Brent: Pouncy, errie piano solos and clean, well‑balanced keyboards; highlights include High Time, Masterpiece, Tons Of Steel and slide work on Little Red Rooster.
band_performance.Mickey: Out‑of‑this‑world drumming with heavy‑hitting moments that drive the post‑drums sequence and add thunderous power to the set.
band_performance.Billy: Provides powerful, heavy‑hitting drum parts alongside Mickey, contributing to the thunderous Drum/Space climax.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
